elements.text: 5604.4: Reviewing appraisal reports, reconsideration
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: of value requested by the Borrower, obtaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsequent appraisal reports and reconciling multiple
elements.label: text

elements.text: opinions of market value (09/03/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal report review requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconsideration of value (ROV) requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements for obtaining subsequent appraisal reports, appraisal desk review reports and
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal field review reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciling multiple opinions of market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage file documentation and delivery data
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Appraisal report review requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Seller responsibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must evaluate the appraisal report to determine whether it meets the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this topic and the Seller’s other Purchase Documents and whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: opinion of market value is credible and adequately supported.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must ensure valuation and related staff, inclusive of third parties (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal management companies, fee-appraisers, review appraisers, underwriters) are
elements.label: text

elements.text: trained to identify prohibited discriminatory practices and appraisal deficiencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including the unacceptable appraisal practices in Section 5603.4) through the valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: review and ROV processes. The Seller must have a process for remediating any
elements.label: text

elements.text: deficiencies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Deficiency remediation and appraisal report rejection
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before rejecting an appraisal report, the Seller must request the appraiser to provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional information and/or address any deficiencies with the appraisal report. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser does not address the Seller’s concerns and the Seller is unable to conclude the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report meets Freddie Mac requirements, the appraisal report must be rejected,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and a new appraisal report must be obtained.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) ROV requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages requiring delivery with an appraisal report, the Seller must have in place
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies and procedures that address requests for an ROV that meet, at a minimum, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements and any requirements required under applicable local, State or federal
elements.label: text

elements.text: law. Freddie Mac’s ROV requirements are minimum standards. If State law or regulation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requires more than Freddie Mac’s guidelines, the State law or regulation controls.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The ROV process must include a review and resolution procedure, including steps for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower(s) to appeal an appraisal report’s findings when the Borrower(s) believes the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report or the appraiser’s opinion of value is unsupported, may be deficient due to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: unacceptable appraisal practice, or reflects discriminatory practices. The Seller remains
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for ensuring that the opinion of market value is credible, and the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: meets the requirements of the Seller’s Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the Seller’s ROV policies and procedures must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide for a disclosure to the Borrower(s) outlining the ROV process upon delivery of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appraisal report to the Borrower(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The disclosure must include instructions for requesting the ROV
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The requirements for Borrower submissions of information are the same as those
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the standardized format for submission to the appraiser as described
elements.label: text

elements.text: below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure the Seller completes its appraisal review before initiating the ROV process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide a standardized format for providing the rationale, requirements and supporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation for the ROV outcome to be communicated to the appraiser. This must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Reporting the Borrower(s) name, property address and the effective date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal, appraiser’s name and date of the ROV submission
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Identifying specific issues and deficiencies in the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Providing detailed information, data or alternative comparable properties (maximum
elements.label: text

elements.text: of five alternative comparables are permitted), including the source of the data (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: multiple listing service listing or publicly available information) and the rationale for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the inclusion of the alternative comparables, information or data, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure the ROV request is accurately completed and includes sufficient detail to warrant
elements.label: text

elements.text: reengagement of the appraiser. If the ROV request is unclear, deficient or requires
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional information, the Seller should remediate with the Borrower(s), as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Instruct the appraiser to deliver a revised appraisal report that includes specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: commentary explaining their conclusions to the ROV request, regardless of whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser determines that changes are not needed to address the issues identified in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: ROV
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define turn-time expectations for communicating results of the ROV to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Specify that one Borrower-initiated ROV is permitted per appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure documentation related to the outcome of the ROV is retained in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note that once a loan is closed, an ROV request is no longer permitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not conflict with Exhibit 35, Appraiser Independence Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If material deficiencies are identified in the appraisal report that are not corrected or
elements.label: text

elements.text: addressed by the appraiser upon request, or if there is evidence of unacceptable appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: practices as outlined in Section 5603.4, the Seller must forward the appraisal report, along
elements.label: text

elements.text: with a summary of findings, to the appropriate appraisal licensing agency or regulatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: board. Additionally, if there are suspected overt violations of antidiscrimination laws, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: lender must report it to the proper local, State or federal agency. In the event of these
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurrences, the lender may obtain a second or subsequent appraisal report in adherence to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requirements and local, State and federal laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Requirements for obtaining subsequent appraisal reports, appraisal desk review
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports and appraisal field review reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 35 provides that the Seller must not order, obtain, use or pay for a subsequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report, in connection with a Mortgage financing transaction unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ There are indicators the initial appraisal report was inaccurate, not credible or in violation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of legal and/or professional standards related to nondiscrimination and such indicators are
elements.label: text

elements.text: clearly and appropriately noted in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The subsequent appraisal is done pursuant to written, pre-established bona fide pre- or
elements.label: text

elements.text: post-funding appraisal review or quality control processes or underwriting guidelines,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and so long as the Seller adheres to a policy of selecting the most reliable appraisal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: rather than the appraisal that states a particular value; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A subsequent appraisal is required by law
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Reconciling multiple opinions of market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the initial appraisal report was not rejected and a subsequent appraisal report, appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: desk review report or appraisal field review report is obtained in compliance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this chapter and the Seller’s other Purchase Documents, the Seller must use
elements.label: text

elements.text: the most credible opinion of market value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Mortgage file documentation and delivery data
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must retain in the Mortgage file copies of all documents used in the valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: analysis, as well as written documentation justifying the Seller’s decision as to which
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report (or appraisal desk review report or appraisal field review report) was used to
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwrite the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The value used to underwrite the Mortgage is the basis for the Seller’s value warranty and is
elements.label: text

elements.text: the value that must be provided to Freddie Mac as part of the delivery data. If the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report used to underwrite the Mortgage can be submitted to the Uniform Collateral Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Portal®
elements.label: text

elements.text: (UCDP®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ), the appraisal report must be submitted to the UCDP and receive a
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Successful” status prior to delivery of the Mortgage. See Section 5606.2 for more
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on UCDP.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5605: Appraisal Requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 5605.1: Appraisal report requirements and appraiser
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: responsibilities (06/04/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal report requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraiser responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Appraisal report requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must meet Freddie Mac’s requirements for property valuation, appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting, and quality. The Seller must ensure the appraiser’s compliance with the Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5604.1 for information on appraisal report forms.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Appraiser responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The description of the Mortgaged Premises must be complete and accurate, and the opinion
elements.label: text

elements.text: of market value of the subject property must be credible and adequately supported. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser must have knowledge and experience appraising in the Market Area in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property is located. An appraisal assignment may be particularly challenging, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser’s knowledge and experience even more important, when the property is in an area
elements.label: text

elements.text: with various property types and land uses (e.g., rural areas).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.2: Definition of market value (06/04/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: An appraisal must be based on the following definition of market value:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The most probable price which a property should bring in a competitive and open market under
elements.label: text

elements.text: all conditions requisite to a fair sale, the buyer and seller, each acting prudently, knowledgeably
elements.label: text

elements.text: and assuming the price is not affected by undue stimulus.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Implicit in this definition is the consummation of a sale as of a specified date and the passing of
elements.label: text

elements.text: title from seller to buyer under conditions whereby:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Buyer and seller are typically motivated
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Both parties are well informed or well advised, and each is acting in what he or she considers
elements.label: text

elements.text: his or her own best interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. A reasonable time is allowed for exposure in the open market
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Payment is made in terms of cash in U.S. dollars or in terms of financial arrangements
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable thereto, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The price represents the normal consideration for the property sold unaffected by special or
elements.label: text

elements.text: creative financing or sales concessions* granted by anyone associated with the sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: *Adjustments to the comparables must be made for special or creative financing or sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: concessions. No adjustments are necessary for those costs which are normally paid by sellers
elements.label: text

elements.text: as a result of tradition or law in a Market Area; these costs are readily identifiable since the
elements.label: text

elements.text: seller pays these costs in virtually all sales transactions. Special or creative financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjustments can be made to the comparable property by comparisons to financing terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: offered by a third-party institutional lender that is not already involved in the property or
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction. Any adjustment should not be calculated on a mechanical dollar-for-dollar cost
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the financing or concession, but the dollar amount of any adjustment should approximate
elements.label: text

elements.text: the market’s reaction to the financing or concessions based on the appraiser’s judgment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The market value estimate of the subject property must not include value assigned to furniture or
elements.label: text

elements.text: any other personal property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.3: Subject property, contract and Neighborhood
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: description (01/26/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identifying the subject property address, legal description, occupancy status, property rights,
elements.label: text

elements.text: listing information and lender/client
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contract analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Neighborhood description and analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Identifying the subject property address, legal description, occupancy status, property
elements.label: text

elements.text: rights, listing information and lender/client
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Subject property address and owner of record
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “Subject” section of the appraisal report must identify the subject property by
elements.label: text

elements.text: providing a complete property address and legal description and by identifying the owner
elements.label: text

elements.text: of public record for the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For appraisal reports that are required to be completed using the Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD), the format of the property address must conform to the United States
elements.label: text

elements.text: Postal Service (USPS) Address Standards in Publication 28. (Refer to Section 5606.1.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Legal description
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac with an appraisal report, the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: must include the legal description of the subject property. If the legal description is
elements.label: text

elements.text: lengthy, the appraiser may attach it as an addendum to the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The legal description used in the Security Instrument, title insurance policy or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidence of title must be in one of the forms listed below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Metes and bounds
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the appraisal report contains a metes and bounds legal description, it should comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the following standards:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The beginning point should be established by a monument located at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: beginning point or by reference to a nearby monument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each boundary of the Mortgaged Premises must be described by its distances and
elements.label: text

elements.text: bearings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: In place of bearings, the interior angle method is acceptable if the beginning
elements.label: text

elements.text: point is on a dedicated public street line or a fixed line on other property or if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: course of the first side can be otherwise properly fixed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The distances, bearings and angles should be taken from a recent instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: survey or a recently recertified instrument survey by a licensed civil engineer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: registered surveyor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Curved courses should be described by data including the length of arc, the radius
elements.label: text

elements.text: of circle for the arc and the chord distance and bearing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: When a survey course is part of a dedicated public street or road line, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: course may be described by indicating the distance and direction the course takes
elements.label: text

elements.text: along the street line from the end of the previous course, if commonly accepted by
elements.label: text

elements.text: private institutional Mortgage investors in the Market Area where the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The legal description should be a single perimeter description of the entire plot
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The subject property must not be divided into parcels unless serving a special
elements.label: text

elements.text: purpose of the Mortgage; however:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The subject property must be divided if the parcel is located on two sides of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: public way
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ It is customary in many areas to describe an easement appurtenant to a fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: simple parcel by using a separate parcel description
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Lot and block
elements.label: text

elements.text: A description composed of lots and/or blocks, including a reference to a recorded
elements.label: text

elements.text: map or plat that shows the lots or blocks, is usually adequate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When all lots or blocks in the description do not appear on the same recorded map or
elements.label: text

elements.text: plat, a reference to the location of the apparently identical sides of lots or blocks in
elements.label: text

elements.text: different recorded maps or plats, fixed in both maps or both plats by the same
elements.label: text

elements.text: monuments (a rare situation), is usually adequate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Additional acceptable forms
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a parcel is bounded on all sides by dedicated streets or alleys, it is acceptable for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the legal description to refer only to the bounding lines of the streets or alleys.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A description of registered property is acceptable if it is in the form required by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: local Torrens Act.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (D)Consistency of legal description
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage, title insurance policy (or substitute evidence of title), survey, lease,
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance policy, property insurance policies and all other documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: pertaining to the Mortgage or the Mortgaged Premises must each have a legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: description consistent with that in the other documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Occupancy status, property rights and listing information
elements.label: text

elements.text: The occupancy status of the property must be identified as either owner, tenant or vacant
elements.label: text

elements.text: as of the effective date of the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The property rights appraised must be reported as either fee simple or leasehold, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report must indicate whether the property is currently offered for sale or was
elements.label: text

elements.text: offered for sale within the 12 months prior to the effective date of the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must state the data source(s) used, offering price(s), date(s) and days
elements.label: text

elements.text: on market for the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Lender/client identification
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must include the name of the lender on the lender/client line. Any
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable appraisal management company must be reported in the appraiser’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification section of the appraisal report form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Contract analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: The contract for sale for the subject property must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sale or contract price
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Date of contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any loan charges to be paid by the property seller, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any financing or sales concessions to be paid by the property seller or any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: interested party to the transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must ensure the complete contract for sale for the subject property is provided to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appraiser with the appraisal request, regardless of whether the appraisal is ordered by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller or another lender.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “Contract” section of the appraisal report must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The results of the appraiser’s analysis of the contract for sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contract price
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Date of contract, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Acknowledgement as to whether the property seller is the owner of public record, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: data source(s) used
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser must have the necessary and appropriate data sources for the Market Area in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the subject property is located. The appraisal report must include the total dollar
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount and a description of any financial assistance (e.g., loan charges, sales concessions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: gift or Down Payment assistance) to be paid by any party on behalf of the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For appraisal reports that are required to be completed using the UAD, the “Contract” section
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the appraisal report must indicate the type of sale for the underlying transaction. Valid
elements.label: text

elements.text: UAD sale types include REO sale, short sale, court-ordered sale, estate sale, relocation sale,
elements.label: text

elements.text: non-arm’s length sale and arm’s length sale. (Refer to Section 5606.1.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Neighborhood description and analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “Neighborhood” section of the appraisal report requires the appraiser to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify the Neighborhood boundaries and describe the Neighborhood characteristics as
elements.label: text

elements.text: either “Urban,” “Suburban” or “Rural”
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Describe the percent built-up as either “Over 75%,” “25-75%” or “Under 25%”
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Describe the growth rate as either “Rapid,” “Stable” or “Slow”
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Report on market conditions, one-unit housing trends, price and age ranges and present
elements.label: text

elements.text: land uses for the properties in the Neighborhood
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Market Area analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by residential properties in urban, suburban or rural Market Areas are
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for delivery to Freddie Mac if the Mortgaged Premises is adequate collateral for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the transaction, based on the value, condition and marketability of the property. Market
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions and Neighborhood or Market Area characteristics vary based on property
elements.label: text

elements.text: location.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must include the appraiser’s explanation of their conclusions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Neighborhood or Market Area characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: Neighborhood or Market Area characteristics that are typical in certain locations may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: exist in other locations; therefore, Neighborhood or Market Area characteristics must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: viewed in the context of the location of the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Urban locations often consist of a variety of different property types that have different
elements.label: text

elements.text: uses. It is not unusual in urban Neighborhoods to find properties with mixed uses, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential properties that also have a commercial use. Rural locations may have
elements.label: text

elements.text: agricultural zoning and/or consist of a variety of different property types and land uses,
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as large sites with outbuildings, farms, ranches and undeveloped land.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When performing the Neighborhood or Market Area analysis, the appraiser must consider
elements.label: text

elements.text: the existence of any of the following in the Neighborhood or Market Area:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mixed-use property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-residential property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-residential land uses, such as agricultural property, undeveloped land or land
elements.label: text

elements.text: under development
elements.label: text

elements.text: The existence of mixed-use properties, non-residential properties or non-residential land
elements.label: text

elements.text: uses in the Neighborhood or Market Area does not make the residential properties in
elements.label: text

elements.text: those locations ineligible as security for Mortgages delivered to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example:
elements.label: text

elements.text: A subject property in a rural area where agricultural activities are prevalent may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible as security for a Mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac if the appraiser determines
elements.label: text

elements.text: the subject property is residential, based on the subject property’s characteristics and land
elements.label: text

elements.text: use.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.3: Subject property (Future effective date
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Legal description
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ownership rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “Subject Property” section of the appraisal report must identify the subject property by
elements.label: text

elements.text: providing a complete physical property address and legal description.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The format of the property address must conform to the United States Postal Service (USPS)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Address Standards in Publication 28. This section also requires the identification of certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: property characteristics (e.g., Planned Unit Development (PUD), Condominium,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative, new construction), number of dwellings, attachment type, existence of an ADU,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership rights and special assessments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Legal description
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac with an appraisal report, the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: must include the legal description of the subject property. If the legal description is lengthy,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appraiser may upload an image file of the legal description in the “Subject Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibits” of the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The legal description used in the Security Instrument, title insurance policy or other evidence
elements.label: text

elements.text: of title, survey, lease, mortgage insurance policy, property insurance policies and all other
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents pertaining to the Mortgage or the Mortgaged Premises must describe the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although encountered in only a few cases, a description of a parcel bounded on all sides by
elements.label: text

elements.text: dedicated streets or alleys can acceptably refer only to the bounding lines of the streets or
elements.label: text

elements.text: alleys.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A description of registered property is acceptable if it is in the form required by the local
elements.label: text

elements.text: Torrens Act.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Metes and bounds
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the appraisal report contains a metes and bounds legal description, it should comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the following standards:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The beginning point should be established by a monument located at the beginning
elements.label: text

elements.text: point or by reference to a nearby monument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each boundary of the Mortgaged Premises must be described by its distances and
elements.label: text

elements.text: bearings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: In place of bearings, the interior angle method is acceptable if the beginning
elements.label: text

elements.text: point is on a dedicated public street line or a fixed line on other property or if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: course of the first side can be otherwise properly fixed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The distances, bearings and angles should be taken from a recent instrument survey or
elements.label: text

elements.text: a recently recertified instrument survey by a licensed civil engineer or registered
elements.label: text

elements.text: surveyor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Curved courses should be described by data including the length of arc, the radius of
elements.label: text

elements.text: circle for the arc and the chord distance and bearing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: When a survey course is part of a dedicated public street or road line, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: course may be described by indicating the distance and direction the course takes
elements.label: text

elements.text: along the street line from the end of the previous course, if commonly accepted by
elements.label: text

elements.text: private institutional Mortgage investors in the Market Area where the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The legal description should be a single perimeter description of the entire plot
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The subject property must not be divided into parcels unless serving a special purpose
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage; however:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The subject property must be divided if the parcel is located on two sides of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: public way
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ It is customary in many areas to describe an easement appurtenant to a fee simple
elements.label: text

elements.text: parcel by using a separate parcel description
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Lot and block
elements.label: text

elements.text: A description composed of lots and/or blocks, including a reference to a recorded map or
elements.label: text

elements.text: plat that shows the lots or blocks, is usually adequate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When all lots or blocks in the description do not appear on the same recorded map or plat,
elements.label: text

elements.text: a reference to the location of the apparently identical sides of lots or blocks in different
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded maps or plats, fixed in both maps or both plats by the same monuments (a rare
elements.label: text

elements.text: situation), is usually adequate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Ownership rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “Ownership Rights” section of the appraisal report describes the ownership and property
elements.label: text

elements.text: rights appraised for the subject property and must be reported as either fee simple or
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasehold. The appraisal report must identify any property rights excluded from the valuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the subject property (e.g., mineral rights, water rights).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.4: Site and location analysis (01/26/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Environmental issues and detrimental conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Property characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “Site” section of the appraisal report must accurately describe the physical
elements.label: text

elements.text: characteristics of the site, site improvements, site view and available utilities, and it must
elements.label: text

elements.text: fully analyze any locational factors affecting the site.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Zoning
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must accurately describe:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The zoning classification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A description of the zoning classification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether the land use of the subject property represents a legal use, a legal non￾conforming use (commonly referred to as grandfathered use), or an illegal use, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is no zoning
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A) Eligible zoning classification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not limit Mortgage purchases to Mortgages secured by properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: with specific zoning classifications; however, the Mortgaged Premises zoning
elements.label: text

elements.text: classification is an important characteristic the Seller must consider when determining
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the Mortgage is eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If a property is zoned for agricultural use, the Seller must ensure the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property is residential in nature, its residential use is a permissible use under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: zoning classification and its use does not primarily involve commercial activities such
elements.label: text

elements.text: as farming or ranching.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Eligible zoning compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The zoning compliance must be either legal or legal non-conforming.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A 1-unit property with an ADU that has illegal zoning compliance may be eligible if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of Section 5601.2 are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A 2- or 3-unit property with an ADU must have legal or legal non-conforming zoning
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A 4-unit property with an ADU is not eligible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises in jurisdictions with no zoning are acceptable security for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages delivered to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgaged Premises with a land use that is legal non-conforming, the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report must reflect any adverse effect the non-conforming use has on the opinion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage is ineligible for sale to Freddie Mac if the Mortgage is secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: property that is subject to coastal tideland, wetland or setback laws or regulations that
elements.label: text

elements.text: prevent the rebuilding or maintenance of the property improvements if they are
elements.label: text

elements.text: damaged or destroyed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Highest and best use
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the Mortgage to be eligible for sale to Freddie Mac, the highest and best use of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property as improved (or as proposed per plans and specifications) must be the
elements.label: text

elements.text: present use.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Utilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must describe the utilities permanently connected to the site and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the impact to value and marketability. The utilities serving the subject property must
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet community standards. The comparable sales should have utilities similar to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When differences in utilities exist between the subject property and the comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales, any adjustments or lack of adjustments made to the comparable sales for
elements.label: text

elements.text: significant differences must be explained in the comments area or on an attached
elements.label: text

elements.text: addendum.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Streets
elements.label: text

elements.text: The subject property must have legal ingress and egress by streets constructed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintained in a manner that meets community standards. Refer to Section 4702.4(e) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements related to access provided by a private road, joint-driveway or easement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report should contain comparable sales with street access, ownership,
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance and materials similar to the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When differences in street access, ownership, maintenance or materials exist between
elements.label: text

elements.text: the subject property and a comparable sale, the appraisal report must include
elements.label: text

elements.text: justification and support for the adjustments, or lack of adjustments, made to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sale. The appraisal report should include an explanation of the effect these
elements.label: text

elements.text: differences have on the subject property’s value or marketability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Site size
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not limit Mortgage purchases based on the size of the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: site. The appraisal report must identify the total area of the site. The appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: should include comparable sales with similar site sizes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When differences in site size exist between the subject property and the comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales, the appraisal report must include an explanation in the comments area of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report or on an attached addendum for any adjustments or lack of adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: made for significant differences in site size between the subject property and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales and the effect these differences have on the subject property’s value or
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Flood hazard area
elements.label: text

elements.text: The flood hazard section of the appraisal report is not required to be completed if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: flood zone is determined by another party, such as a non-appraiser on the staff of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller, a surveyor or a specialized flood zone determination company.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the property is in a “Special Flood Hazard Area” as identified by the Federal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Emergency Management Agency through the National Flood Insurance Program, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report must describe any impacts this has on the subject property’s value or
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 4703.3 for flood zone determination and flood insurance requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Environmental issues and detrimental conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Impact of Contaminated Sites, Hazardous Substances and other adverse conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller knows of the existence of a Contaminated Site in the Neighborhood where
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property is located, it must inform the appraiser of the Contaminated Site when
elements.label: text

elements.text: ordering the appraisal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must identify any known environmental issues, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contaminated Sites, Hazardous Substances or other adverse conditions, that affect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property or the Neighborhood in which the property is located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must include adjustments to reflect any impact on market value and
elements.label: text

elements.text: commentary on any effect to the marketability of the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of conditions that require analysis and commentary include, but are not limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Presence of asbestos, urea-formaldehyde or any similar insulation in the dwelling
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Proximity of the subject property to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Contaminated Site
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Ground water contamination, chemical or petroleum spills or other Hazardous
elements.label: text

elements.text: Substances that are expected to impact the area for more than one year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Areas that may affect the value or marketability of the subject property, including,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Industrial sites
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Waste or water treatment facilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Commercial establishments (other than retail establishments that serve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Neighborhood)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Airport approach paths
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Floodplains
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Landslide areas
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Detrimental conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must identify the presence of detrimental conditions, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: expansive soils, underground mines or subsidence, in the immediate area or on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property site. If the detrimental condition(s) is located on the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: site, the appraisal report must be made “subject to” an inspection by an appropriately
elements.label: text

elements.text: licensed professional or another person trained in the particular field of concern to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine if a repair or alteration is required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser must consider the effect the condition(s) may have on estimating the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property’s market value and marketability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any appraisal report that is made subject to an inspection(s) due to a detrimental
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition(s), the Seller must include in the Mortgage file evidence of repair, alteration or
elements.label: text

elements.text: an inspection report indicates the condition does not require or alteration. (See Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.8 for additional information related to appraisal reports completed “subject to” and
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.4: Site (Future effective date 11/02/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Site characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Environmental issues and detrimental conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Utilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Hazard zones
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Site characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “Site” section of the appraisal report must accurately describe the characteristics of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: site, such as size, number of parcels, property access, site influences, view, site features, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: utilities. The appraisal report must identify any impact the site features or view have on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: value and marketability of the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Site size and parcels
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not limit Mortgage purchases based on the size of the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: site. The appraisal report must identify the total area of the site. If the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises is situated on more than one parcel, the appraisal report must identify whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: the parcels have at least one common boundary or an object or element separates the
elements.label: text

elements.text: parcels.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Mortgaged Premises is situated on two or more parcels:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The parcels must be adjoining.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: Parcels that would otherwise be adjoined but are separated by a road
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., a waterfront property where the parcel without the residence offers access to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the water) are eligible if the parcel without the residence is non-buildable (but may
elements.label: text

elements.text: include non-residential improvements such as a garage or dock) and the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file contains evidence that the parcel without the residence cannot be improved with
elements.label: text

elements.text: a dwelling.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises must contain only one residence. (An ADU is allowed on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the parcel that contains the residence if the eligibility requirements of Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5601.2 are met.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be a valid First Lien on each parcel
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each parcel must have the same basic zoning (e.g., residential, agricultural)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each parcel must be conveyed in its entirety
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The site description must accurately describe the land and any improvements
elements.label: text

elements.text: included on each parcel
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The residence or improvements may be situated across lot lines
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-residential improvements (e.g., garages, outbuildings) are acceptable on any
elements.label: text

elements.text: parcel
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Zoning
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must accurately describe:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The zoning classification code as defined by the local governing authority (e.g., R-1,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A description of the zoning classification code and the details of the classification
elements.label: text

elements.text: code as used and defined by the local governing authority (e.g., residential single￾family dwellings on 1-acre lots, residential 2-4 family dwellings)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The level of compliance of the site or improvements with zoning regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Acceptable responses include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Legal use
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Legal non-conforming use (any illegal use that is grandfathered or allowed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: special exception)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Illegal use
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ There is no zoning
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A) Eligible zoning classification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not limit Mortgage purchases to Mortgages secured by properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: with specific zoning classifications; however, the Mortgaged Premises zoning
elements.label: text

elements.text: classification is an important characteristic the Seller must consider when determining
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether the Mortgage is eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If a property is zoned for agricultural use, the Seller must ensure the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property is residential in nature, its residential use is a permissible use under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: zoning classification and its use does not primarily involve commercial activities such
elements.label: text

elements.text: as farming or ranching.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Eligible zoning compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The zoning compliance must be either legal or legal non-conforming.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A 1-unit property with an ADU that has illegal zoning compliance may be eligible if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of Section 5601.2 are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A 2- or 3-unit property with an ADU must have legal or legal non-conforming zoning
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A 4-unit property with an ADU is not eligible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises in jurisdictions with no zoning are acceptable security for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages delivered to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgaged Premises with a land use that is legal non-conforming, the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report must reflect any adverse effect the non-conforming use has on the opinion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage is ineligible for sale to Freddie Mac if the Mortgage is secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: property that is subject to coastal tideland, wetland or setback laws or regulations that
elements.label: text

elements.text: prevent the rebuilding or maintenance of the property improvements if they are
elements.label: text

elements.text: damaged or destroyed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Property access
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgaged Premises must have legal ingress and egress by streets constructed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintained in a manner that meets community standards. Refer to Section 4702.4(e) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements related to access provided by a private road, joint-driveway or easement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report should contain comparable sales with street access, ownership,
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance and materials similar to the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Environmental issues and detrimental conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Impact of Contaminated Sites, Hazardous Substances and other adverse conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller knows of the existence of a Contaminated Site in the Neighborhood, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must inform the appraiser of the Contaminated Site when ordering the appraisal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must identify any known environmental issues, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contaminated Sites, Hazardous Substances or other adverse conditions, that affect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property or are within the vicinity of the property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must include adjustments to reflect any impact on market value and
elements.label: text

elements.text: commentary on any effect to the marketability of the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of conditions that require analysis and commentary include, but are not limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Hazardous Above Ground Storage Tank
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Hazardous Substances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Landfill
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Radon
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Slush Pit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Soil Contamination
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Superfund Site
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Underground Storage Tank
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Water Contamination
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Detrimental conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must identify the presence of detrimental conditions, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: expansive soils, underground mines or subsidence, in the immediate area or on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property site. If the detrimental condition(s) is located on the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: site, the appraisal report must be made “subject to” an inspection by an appropriately
elements.label: text

elements.text: licensed professional or another person trained in the particular field of concern to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine if a repair or alteration is required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser must consider the effect the condition(s) may have on the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property’s market value and marketability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any appraisal report that is made subject to an inspection(s) due to a detrimental
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition(s), the Seller must include in the Mortgage file evidence of repair, alteration or
elements.label: text

elements.text: an inspection report indicates the condition does not require or alteration. (See Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.9 for additional information related to appraisal reports completed “subject to” and
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Utilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must describe the utilities that are permanently connected to the site and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the impact of the utilities on the value and marketability of the subject property. The utilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: that serve the subject property must meet community standards. The comparable sales should
elements.label: text

elements.text: have utilities that are similar to the subject property’s utilities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When differences exist between the utilities that serve subject property and those that serve
elements.label: text

elements.text: the comparable sales, any adjustments or lack of adjustments to the comparable sales must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: explained in the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Hazard zones
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the subject property is in a hazard zone (i.e., flood zone, lava zone), the appraiser must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include that information in the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the flood zone determination is made by another party other than the appraiser, such as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: non-appraiser on the staff of the Seller, a surveyor or a specialized flood zone determination
elements.label: text

elements.text: company, the flood hazard section of the appraisal report is not required to be completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the property is in a “Special Flood Hazard Area” (SFHA) as identified by the Federal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) through the National Flood Insurance Program
elements.label: text

elements.text: (NFIP), the appraisal report must describe any impacts of the SFHA on the subject property’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: value or marketability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 4703.3 for flood zone determination and flood insurance requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Property restrictions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must identify any property restrictions or encumbrances that affect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property and identify the impact of such restrictions or encumbrances on the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property including commentary describing any impact to the subject property’s value or
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.5: Improvements analysis (06/03/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property condition and quality of construction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Required permits
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unusual floor plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rehabilitated or renovated property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-conformity to the Neighborhood and non-traditional or unique property types
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Outbuildings on a property
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Property condition and quality of construction
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must contain an accurate description of the improvements and describe
elements.label: text

elements.text: any factors that may affect the market value or marketability of the subject property. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser is responsible for reporting the condition and quality that best describe the overall
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition and quality of the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For appraisal reports that are required to be completed using the Uniform Appraisal Dataset
elements.label: text

elements.text: (UAD), the appraiser must use the condition and quality ratings and the level of updating
elements.label: text

elements.text: definitions identified in Exhibit 36, Condition and Quality Ratings and Level of Updating
elements.label: text

elements.text: Definitions. For appraisal reports that are not required to be completed using the UAD, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: UAD specifications may be used to the extent they are applicable to the particular appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The condition and quality ratings must be based on a holistic view of the property and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements. When selecting the condition and quality ratings, the appraiser must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consider all improvements to determine overall condition and quality ratings and select
elements.label: text

elements.text: the ratings that best reflect a holistic view of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If any portion of the improvements has a quality of construction consistent with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Q6 quality rating, the property must be identified with a Q6 quality rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If any portion of the improvements is impacted by one or more deficiencies that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: consistent with a C5 or C6 condition rating, the property must be identified with a C5
elements.label: text

elements.text: or C6 condition rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Describe the subject property on an absolute basis as of the effective date of the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report, meaning the property must be rated on its own merits.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ratings should not be selected on a relative basis, meaning a rating is not selected based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on how the property relates or compares to other properties. The condition and quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratings for comparable properties must be made on an absolute basis and reflect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition and quality of the comparable property as of the date of sale of the comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ As necessary, provide additional commentary, descriptions and explanations to enable the
elements.label: text

elements.text: intended users of the appraisal report to understand the property condition and quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Properties consistent with a Q6 quality rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgaged Premises consistent with a Q6 rating is not acceptable collateral to
elements.label: text

elements.text: secure a Mortgage sold to Freddie Mac unless all issues that caused the property to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: rated with a Q6 quality rating are cured before delivery of the Mortgage to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In such cases, when an appraisal report is delivered with the Mortgage, the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report must be completed “subject to” and the reported quality rating must reflect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: hypothetical condition that would exist if the repairs or alterations were completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5605.8 for information related to appraisal reports completed “subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: to” repairs or alterations and PDRs with required repairs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples that are consistent with a Q6 quality rating include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Quality such that the property is not habitable as a year-round residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimal or non-existent electrical, plumbing and/or other mechanical systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Substandard additions to the original structure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other quality related deficiencies that make the property unacceptable to typical
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchasers in the Market Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exception: Issues that caused the property to have a Q6 quality rating may be cured after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Settlement Date for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ CHOICERenovation® Mortgages that meet the requirements in Section 4607.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: when the issues are cured as part of the completion of the renovation(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ CHOICEReno eXPress® Mortgages that meet the requirements in Section 4607.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: when the issues are cured as part of the completion of the renovation(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Properties with deficiencies consistent with a C5 or C6 condition rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgaged Premises with deficiencies consistent with a C5 or C6 condition rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: is not acceptable collateral to secure a Mortgage sold to Freddie Mac unless all
elements.label: text

elements.text: issues that caused the property to be rated with a C5 or C6 condition rating are cured
elements.label: text

elements.text: before delivery of the Mortgage to Freddie Mac. In such cases, when an appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: is delivered with the Mortgage, the appraisal report must be completed “subject to” and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the reported condition rating must reflect the hypothetical condition that would exist if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the repairs or alterations were completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5605.8 for information related to appraisal reports completed “subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: to” repairs or alterations and PDRs with required repairs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of deficiencies that are consistent with C5 or C6 rating include, but are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Active roof leak(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Incomplete construction or remodel (including missing or severely damaged cabinets
elements.label: text

elements.text: or countertops, missing plumbing fixtures in the kitchen or bathroom(s), etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Missing or damaged exterior components that would allow water or other elements to
elements.label: text

elements.text: enter the dwelling
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Missing or severely damaged interior drywall or plaster such that the wall framing is
elements.label: text

elements.text: exposed (excluding basements)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Standing water in the property, water seepage/intrusion or significant plumbing leaks
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Uncapped wiring
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Curled, cupped or missing roof shingles
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Missing or severely damaged floor coverings in the majority of the living area
elements.label: text

elements.text: (excluding basements)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Damaged or failing foundations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A mechanical system that is non-functional, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A sanitary system with evidence of failure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Deficiencies that caused the property to have a C5 condition rating may be cured
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Settlement Date for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Mortgages secured by properties with incomplete improvements that meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 5601.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ CHOICERenovation Mortgages that meet the requirements in Section 4607.1(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ GreenCHOICE Mortgages® that meet the requirements in Section 4606.3(a)
elements.label: text
