elements.text: 5605.6: Sales comparison approach (01/26/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section and Section 5605.7.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requires that all appraisal reports include a sales comparison approach. This section
elements.label: text

elements.text: contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales and financing concessions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Market Area analysis and market condition adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Location
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ View
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condition and quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Selection of comparable sales and analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sale and listing history
elements.label: text

elements.text: For special appraisal requirements for HeritageOne® Mortgages, see Section 4504.9.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each comparable sale must be analyzed for similarities and differences between it and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property. When the appraiser’s analysis concludes an adjustment is necessary, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report must include adjustments for differences and indicate the dollar amount of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the adjustment to reflect the value of the differences to the market.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must contain an explanation of the basis and rationale for all adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or, if necessary, lack of adjustments).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comparable sales must be adjusted to the subject property except for sales and financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: concessions that must be adjusted based on their effect on the sales price of the comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale. Large adjustments typically occur in rural markets, and with unique properties, due to
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited market activity. Freddie Mac does not have limitations on gross or net adjustment
elements.label: text

elements.text: percentages. See subsection (c) below for market condition adjustment requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Sales and financing concessions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must identify how the sales were verified and whether concessions were
elements.label: text

elements.text: granted.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sales or financing concessions may be offered by interested parties to the transaction (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the builder, developer, property seller or real estate agent).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Adjustments to the comparable sales must be made for special or creative financing or sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: concessions. No adjustments are necessary for costs that are normally paid by property
elements.label: text

elements.text: sellers as a result of tradition or law in a Market Area; these costs are readily identifiable
elements.label: text

elements.text: since the property seller pays these costs in virtually all sales transactions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Adjustments to a comparable sale with a concession(s) must reflect the actual effect on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales price of that comparable sale; that effect may be equal to, lower than or higher than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: actual amount (or value) of the concession.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For Seller treatment of concessions, see Section 5501.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Market Area analysis and market condition adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: The market trend (increasing, stable or declining) must be based on factual data from
elements.label: text

elements.text: information sources, such as, but not limited to, market data, home price indices, multiple
elements.label: text

elements.text: listing services, public records and/or models. The market trend identified in the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report must reflect the overall movement of the market based on a minimum of 12 months of
elements.label: text

elements.text: data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A specific market condition adjustment to a comparable sale may differ from the identified
elements.label: text

elements.text: market trend since the determination of whether to make an adjustment to a comparable sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: is based on market changes between the contract date of the comparable sale and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective date of the appraisal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: The 12-month trend indicates a positive overall trend, although the market was
elements.label: text

elements.text: stable (or declining) between the contract date of the comparable sale and the effective
elements.label: text

elements.text: date of the appraisal report. (See Exhibit 44, Market Condition Adjustments.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comparable sales with a contract date that is recent in relation to the effective date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report may not have a market condition adjustment, given the inability to identify a
elements.label: text

elements.text: change in the market.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must contain the market analysis that supports the market trend and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjustments made for market conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Location
elements.label: text

elements.text: For appraisal report forms that are required to be completed using the UAD, the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report form must include a rating of the location of the subject property and each comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale by providing a rating of either “Neutral,” “Beneficial” or “Adverse.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: The location rating (which will be abbreviated as N, B, or A in the appraisal report form)
elements.label: text

elements.text: describes the overall effect of the location of the subject property within the Market Area on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the value and marketability of the subject property and is not a rating of the overall Market
elements.label: text

elements.text: Area. See Appendix D – Field Specific Standardization Requirements of the Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisal Dataset Specification (“UAD Specification”) for additional requirements regarding
elements.label: text

elements.text: location.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rural locations often have less real estate sales activity than more populated areas. Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales in rural locations often involve a variety of property types and may have relatively large
elements.label: text

elements.text: parcels compared to other locations. Given the potential challenges with appraising
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties in rural Market Areas, the appraiser must be knowledgeable about the varying
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions that characterize properties in a particular geographic area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When appraising properties in rural Market Areas, the appraisal report may contain older
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales, comparable sales that are a considerable distance from the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: or comparable sales that are not ideally similar to the subject property. The appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: must include justification and support in the appraisal report for the use of any such
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales and make appropriate adjustments to account for the differences between
elements.label: text

elements.text: the comparable sales and the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the subject property is a ranch-style home on a large parcel of land (e.g., 44
elements.label: text

elements.text: acres), the most relevant comparable sales may be two-story homes on smaller parcels
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., 6–12 acres) that are some distance from the subject property (e.g., 8–18 miles
elements.label: text

elements.text: away).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) View
elements.label: text

elements.text: For appraisal report forms that are required to be completed using the UAD, the overall view
elements.label: text

elements.text: associated with the subject property and each comparable sale must be rated as either
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Neutral,” “Beneficial” or “Adverse.” The UAD view rating (which will be abbreviated as N,
elements.label: text

elements.text: B or A in the appraisal) describes the overall effect of the view associated with the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property on the value and marketability of the subject property. See Appendix D – Field
elements.label: text

elements.text: Specific Standardization Requirements of the UAD Specification for additional requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding view.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 5606.1 for additional information related to UAD requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Condition and quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: Adjustments must be made for differences in condition and quality between the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property and each comparable sale The appraisal report may contain an adjustment for
elements.label: text

elements.text: differences in quality and condition between the subject property and a comparable property,
elements.label: text

elements.text: even though the properties have the same UAD quality or condition rating. In this case, an
elements.label: text

elements.text: explanation must be provided in the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 5605.5 for information related to property condition and quality of
elements.label: text

elements.text: construction, Section 5606.1 for information related to UAD requirements and Exhibit 36,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condition and Quality Ratings and Level of Updating Definitions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Selection of comparable sales and analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must contain a minimum of three comparable sales as part of the sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparison approach. The appraisal report may include more than three comparable sales,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including contract sales (pending sales) and/or current listings, to support the opinion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report should include comparable sales that closed within 12 months; however,
elements.label: text

elements.text: older comparable sales may be used. The appraisal report must provide commentary on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reasons for using any comparable sales that are more than six months old.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The proper selection of comparable properties minimizes both the need for, and the size of,
elements.label: text

elements.text: any adjustments. This typically includes characteristics such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Finished area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Room count
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Site
elements.label: text

elements.text: When choosing comparable sales, it’s important to identify the Neighborhood in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property is located and determine whether the Neighborhood represents the Market
elements.label: text

elements.text: Area or the Market Area is larger than the Neighborhood. The appraiser may need to use
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales from outside the subject property’s Market Area, and, in such cases,
elements.label: text

elements.text: location adjustments may be necessary. When comparable sales are located outside the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Market Area, the appraisal report must contain commentary explaining the rationale for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: selected comparable sales.
elements.label: text

elements.text: There may be no similar comparable sales for a subject property because of the uniqueness of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the subject property or other conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comparable sales may be taken from a competing Market Area if they are better than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales in the subject’s Market Area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Comparable sale requirements for properties in established subdivisions, established
elements.label: text

elements.text: Planned Unit Developments (PUDs) and Established Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: For properties in established subdivisions, units in established PUDs or units in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Established Condominium Projects, the appraisal report should contain comparable sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: from within the subject subdivision or project when they are the best indicators of value
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5705.8 for specific requirements regarding selection of comparable sales for
elements.label: text

elements.text: units in a Cooperative Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Comparable sale requirements for properties in new subdivisions, new PUDs and
elements.label: text

elements.text: recently converted or New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: To demonstrate the marketability and develop an opinion of market value for properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: in new subdivisions, units in new PUDs or units in recently converted or New
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects, the appraisal report must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. At least one comparable sale from inside the subject subdivision, PUD or project,
elements.label: text

elements.text: when available. Additionally:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The comparable sale from inside the subject subdivision, PUD or project can be a
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale by the builder or developer of the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If there are no closed comparable sales from inside the subject subdivision, PUD
elements.label: text

elements.text: or project, contract sales from inside the subject subdivision, PUD or project may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be used to satisfy this requirement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The use of contract sales must be in addition to the three actual closed sales from
elements.label: text

elements.text: outside the subject subdivision, PUD or project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the subject subdivision, PUD or project is so new that a closed sale or a contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale is not available, comparable sales from outside the subject subdivision, PUD
elements.label: text

elements.text: or project may be used.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In this case, the appraisal report must contain commentary on the marketability of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the new subdivision, PUD or project and justification supporting the use of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales from outside the new subdivision, PUD or project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. At least one comparable sale from outside the subject subdivision, PUD or project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resales from inside the subject subdivision or project are preferable as comparable sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: and should be given significant consideration, as they provide a reliable indicator of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value of units within the subdivision, PUD or project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least two comparable sales must be sales in which the builder or developer of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property was not involved in the sale transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5705.8 for specific requirements regarding selection of comparable sales for
elements.label: text

elements.text: units in a Cooperative Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Sale and listing history
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must demonstrate the appraiser researched, verified and analyzed:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any current agreement for sale for the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any offering for sale of the subject property in the 12 months before the effective date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any sales or transfers of the subject property in the three years before the effective date
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any sales or transfers of each comparable sale in the year before the date of sale of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s determination of the acceptability of each appraisal should include an analysis of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sale and listing history. The Seller must confirm the sale price trend relative to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser’s opinion of market value is reasonable and representative of the market.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchase transactions, the Seller should analyze the appraisal report and the current
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract for sale for the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For both purchase and refinance transactions, the Seller’s underwriting analysis of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report should include any current listing or offering for sale for the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property, the sales history of the subject property and comparable sales and the current
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership of the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To reduce the Seller’s risk of liability resulting from fraudulent or inaccurate appraisals, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller should analyze the subject property and comparable sales and evaluate the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: elapsed between the date(s) the property was acquired and the date(s) resold, or the date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the current resale contract, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the sales history of the subject property or comparable sales indicates current or prior sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: prices may be excessive, and resale dates occurred shortly after the property seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: acquisition of the property, the appraisal report should provide evidence to justify and
elements.label: text

elements.text: support a rapidly appreciating real estate market, significant improvements that resulted in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponding increase in the property value or a previous sale that was below market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: due to a distress or tax sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.6: Market Area, sales contract, sale and listing
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: history (Future effective date 11/02/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Market Area description and analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales contract analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sale and listing history
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Market Area description and analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “Market” section of the appraisal report requires the appraiser to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define the Market Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify properties in the Market Area that directly compete with the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: (listings, pending sales, closed sales) and the housing trends (demand, supply and
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketing time) in the Market Area
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Market Area analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by residential properties in urban, suburban or rural Market Areas are
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for delivery to Freddie Mac if the Mortgaged Premises is adequate collateral for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the transaction, based on the value, condition and marketability of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises. Market conditions and Neighborhood or Market Area characteristics vary
elements.label: text

elements.text: based on property location.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must include the appraiser’s explanation of their conclusions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Neighborhood or Market Area characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: Neighborhood or Market Area characteristics that are typical in certain locations may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: exist in other locations; therefore, Neighborhood or Market Area characteristics must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: viewed in the context of the location of the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Urban locations often consist of a variety of different property types that have different
elements.label: text

elements.text: uses. It is not unusual in urban Neighborhoods to find properties with mixed uses, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential properties that also have a commercial use. Rural locations may have
elements.label: text

elements.text: agricultural zoning and/or consist of a variety of property types and land uses, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: large sites with outbuildings, farms, ranches and undeveloped land.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When performing the Neighborhood or Market Area analysis, the appraiser must consider
elements.label: text

elements.text: the existence of any of the following in the Neighborhood or Market Area:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mixed-use property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-residential property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-residential land uses, such as agricultural property, undeveloped land or land
elements.label: text

elements.text: under development
elements.label: text

elements.text: The existence of mixed-use properties, non-residential properties or non-residential land
elements.label: text

elements.text: uses in the Neighborhood or Market Area does not make the residential properties in
elements.label: text

elements.text: those locations ineligible as security for Mortgages delivered to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example:
elements.label: text

elements.text: A subject property in a rural area where agricultural activities are prevalent may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible as security for a Mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac if the appraiser determines
elements.label: text

elements.text: the subject property is residential, based on the subject property’s characteristics and land
elements.label: text

elements.text: use.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Sales contract analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: The sales contract for the subject property must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sale or contract price
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Date of contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any loan charges to be paid by the property seller, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any financing or sales concessions to be paid by the property seller or any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: interested party to the transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must review the sales contract for the subject property and ensure the complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales contract is provided to the appraiser.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The “Sales Contract” section of the appraisal report must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The results of the appraiser’s analysis of the sales contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contract price
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Date of contract, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether the transaction appears to be arm’s length, the transfer terms, and whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: personal property is being conveyed
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must include the total dollar amount and a description of any financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance (e.g., loan charges, sales concessions, gift or Down Payment assistance) to be paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: by any party on behalf of the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Sale and listing history
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must demonstrate that the appraiser researched, verified, and analyzed:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any current agreement for sale of the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any offering for sale of the subject property in the 12 months before the effective date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any sales or transfers of the subject property during the three years before the effective
elements.label: text

elements.text: date of the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any sales or transfers of each comparable sale during the year before the date of sale of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the comparable sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s determination of the acceptability of each appraisal should include an analysis of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the sale and listing history. The Seller must confirm that the sale price trend relative to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser’s opinion of market value is reasonable and representative of the market.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For both purchase and refinance transactions, the Seller’s underwriting analysis of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report should include any current listing or offering for sale for the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property, the sales history of the subject property and comparable sales, and the current
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership of the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To reduce the Seller’s risk of liability resulting from fraudulent or inaccurate appraisals, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller should analyze the subject property and comparable sales and evaluate the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: elapsed between the date(s) the property was acquired, and the date(s) resold, or the date of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the current resale contract, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the sales history of the subject property or comparable sales indicates current or prior sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: prices may be excessive, and resale dates occurred shortly after the property seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: acquisition of the property, the appraisal report should provide evidence to justify and
elements.label: text

elements.text: support a rapidly appreciating real estate market, significant improvements that resulted in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponding increase in the property value or a previous sale that was below market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: due to a distress or tax sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.7: Cost and income approaches and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: reconciliation of market value (02/04/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5605.8.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cost approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Income approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciliation of market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must include any approach to value that is necessary for credible results,
elements.label: text

elements.text: even if the Seller did not request it. The Seller may request the appraiser to develop and report
elements.label: text

elements.text: the cost approach or the income approach, even if not required for the transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Cost approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: The cost approach is required for appraisals of Manufactured Homes but no other property
elements.label: text

elements.text: type. (See Section 5703.9(e) for Manufactured Home requirements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: In Market Areas with unique property styles, a lack of comparable sales or the presence of
elements.label: text

elements.text: unique features (such as outbuildings), the cost approach can provide support for adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: made in the sales comparison approach.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The cost approach may be appropriate when appraising properties that are:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ New or proposed construction (i.e., the construction has not started and the description of
elements.label: text

elements.text: construction to be completed is based on plans and specifications and/or similar
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Under renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unique because of property features (e.g., outbuildings, stables, pole-barns, shops)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unique because of their styles or construction methods (e.g., barn conversions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“barndominiums”), “shouses” (living-space and work/storage combinations), berm
elements.label: text

elements.text: homes, log homes or geodesic dome dwellings), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not typical for the Market Area or have functional obsolescence
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisal reports that rely solely on the cost approach for the opinion of market value are
elements.label: text

elements.text: unacceptable, except for appraisals completed for a HeritageOne® Mortgage. For special
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal requirements related to HeritageOne Mortgages, see Section 4504.9.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not require an estimate of remaining economic life.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Income approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: The income approach is required for appraisals of 2- to 4-unit properties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisals that rely solely on the income approach for the opinion of market value are
elements.label: text

elements.text: unacceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Reconciliation of market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: The data, valuation approaches and information presented in the appraisal report must justify
elements.label: text

elements.text: and support the appraiser’s opinion of market value. The appraisal report must contain an
elements.label: text

elements.text: explanation of how the final value conclusion was determined, and the rationale must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: consistent with the comments, conclusions and assumptions stated throughout the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The reconciliation must contain any conditions of the appraisal on which the final opinion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value is based.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the subject transaction involves sales or financing concessions, the appraiser’s opinion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value must reflect the value of the subject property without the concessions. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser must provide the dollar value of the concessions as a comment in the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5605.7: Sales comparison approach (Future effective
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Freddie Mac requires that all appraisal reports include a sales comparison approach. This section
elements.label: text

elements.text: contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comparable sales data sources and verification sources
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales and financing concessions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Market Area analysis and market condition adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Location
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Quality and condition
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Selection of comparable sales and analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: For special appraisal requirements for HeritageOne® Mortgages, see Section 4504.9.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Comparable sales data sources and verification sources
elements.label: text

elements.text: The data source(s) and the source(s) of verification for the comparable sales must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: identified in the Sales Comparison Approach section of the URAR in the data source field.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of data source(s) and verification source(s) include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assessor record
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Builder or developer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium questionnaire
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exterior inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interior inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Previous appraisal file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Multiple listing service (MLS)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Homeowners association
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property owner
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Real estate gent
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there is a unique identifier associated with the source of the data or verification (e.g., MLS
elements.label: text

elements.text: #123456), it must also be included in the data source field.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser may obtain source data from an interested party to the subject transaction if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: data is verified by a disinterested party.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each comparable sale must be analyzed for similarities and differences between it and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property. When the appraiser’s analysis concludes an adjustment is necessary, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report must include adjustments for differences and indicate the dollar amount of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the adjustment to reflect the value of the differences to the market.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must contain an explanation of the basis and rationale for all adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or, if necessary, lack of adjustments).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comparable sales must be adjusted to the subject property, except for sales and financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: concessions that must be adjusted based on their effect on the sales price of the comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale. Large adjustments typically occur in rural markets, and with unique properties, due to
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited market activity. Freddie Mac does not have limitations on gross or net adjustment
elements.label: text

elements.text: percentages. See subsection (c) below for market condition adjustment requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Sales and financing concessions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must identify how the comparable sales were verified and whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: concessions were granted. Sales or financing concessions may be offered by interested
elements.label: text

elements.text: parties to the transaction (e.g., the builder, developer, property seller or real estate agent).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Adjustments to the comparable sales must be made for special or creative financing or sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: concessions. No adjustments are necessary for costs that are normally paid by property
elements.label: text

elements.text: sellers because of tradition or law in a Market Area; these costs are readily identifiable since
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property seller pays the costs in virtually all sales transactions in the Market Area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If concessions were granted in a comparable sale, the appraisal report must include
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjustments that reflect the effect of the concessions on the sales price of that comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale, which may be equal to, lower than or higher than the amount (or value) of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: concessions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For Seller treatment of concessions, see Section 5501.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Market Area analysis and market condition adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: The market price trend must be based on factual data from information sources, such as, but
elements.label: text

elements.text: not limited to, market data, home price indices, MLS, public records and/or models. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: market price trend identified in the appraisal report must reflect the overall movement of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: market based on a minimum of 12 months of data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A specific market condition adjustment to a comparable sale may differ from the identified
elements.label: text

elements.text: market price trend since the determination whether to make an adjustment to a comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale is based on market changes between the contract date of the comparable sale and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective date of the appraisal.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: The 12-month price trend indicates a positive overall trend, although the
elements.label: text

elements.text: market was stable (or declining) between the contract date of the comparable sale and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective date of the appraisal report (see Exhibit 44, Market Condition Adjustments).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comparable sales with a contract date that is recent in relation to the effective date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report might not have a market condition adjustment, given the inability to identify
elements.label: text

elements.text: a change in the market.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must contain the market analysis that supports the market price trend
elements.label: text

elements.text: and any adjustments made for market conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rural locations often have less real estate sales activity than more populated areas. Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales in rural locations often involve a variety of property types and may have relatively large
elements.label: text

elements.text: parcels compared to other locations. Given the potential challenges with appraising
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties in rural Market Areas, the appraiser must be knowledgeable about the varying
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions that characterize properties in a particular geographic area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When appraising properties in rural Market Areas, the appraisal report may contain older
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales, comparable sales that are a considerable distance from the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: or comparable sales that are not ideally similar to the subject property. The appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: must include justification and support in the appraisal report for the use of the comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales and make appropriate adjustments to account for the differences between the
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales and the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the subject property is a ranch-style home on a large parcel of land (e.g., 44
elements.label: text

elements.text: acres), the most relevant comparable sales may be two-story homes on smaller parcels
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., 6–12 acres) that are some distance from the subject property (e.g., 8–18 miles
elements.label: text

elements.text: away).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Quality and condition
elements.label: text

elements.text: Adjustments must be made for differences in quality and condition between the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property and each comparable sale. The appraisal report may contain an adjustment for
elements.label: text

elements.text: differences in quality and condition between the subject property and a comparable property,
elements.label: text

elements.text: even though the properties have the same Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD) quality or
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition rating. In this case, an explanation must be provided in the Reconciliation of Sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comparison Approach section of the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 5605.5 for information related to property condition and quality of
elements.label: text

elements.text: construction, Section 5606.1 for information related to the UAD requirements and Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 36, Condition and Quality Ratings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Selection of comparable sales and analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must contain a minimum of three comparable sales as part of the sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparison approach. The appraisal report may include more than three comparable sales,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including contract sales (pending sales) and/or current listings, to support the opinion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report should include comparable sales that closed within 12 months; provided,
elements.label: text

elements.text: however, older comparable sales may be used. The appraisal report must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: commentary on the reasons for using any comparable sales that are more than six months old.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The proper selection of comparable properties minimizes both the need for, and the size of,
elements.label: text

elements.text: any adjustments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This typically includes characteristics such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Finished area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Room count
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Site
elements.label: text

elements.text: When choosing comparable sales, it’s important to identify the Neighborhood in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property is located and determine whether the Neighborhood represents the Market
elements.label: text

elements.text: Area or the Market Area is larger than the Neighborhood. The appraiser may need to use
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales from outside the subject property’s Market Area and, in such cases,
elements.label: text

elements.text: location adjustments may be necessary. When comparable sales are located outside the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Market Area, the appraisal report must contain commentary explaining the rationale for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: selected comparable sales.
elements.label: text

elements.text: There may be no similar comparable sales for a subject property because of the uniqueness of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the subject property or other conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comparable sales may be taken from a competing Market Area if they are better than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales in the subject property’s Market Area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Comparable sale requirements for properties in established subdivisions, established
elements.label: text

elements.text: Planned Unit Developments (PUDs) and Established Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: For properties in established subdivisions, units in established PUDs or units in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Established Condominium Projects, the appraisal report should contain comparable sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: from within the subject subdivision or project when they are the best indicators of value
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5705.8 for specific requirements regarding selection of comparable sales for
elements.label: text

elements.text: units in a Cooperative Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Comparable sale requirements for properties in new subdivisions, new PUDs and
elements.label: text

elements.text: recently converted or New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: To demonstrate the marketability and develop an opinion of market value for properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: in new subdivisions, units in new PUDs or units in recently converted or New
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects, the appraisal report must contain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. At least one comparable sale from inside the subject subdivision, PUD or project,
elements.label: text

elements.text: when available. Additionally:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The comparable sale from inside the subject subdivision, PUD or project can be a
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale by the builder or developer of the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If there are no closed comparable sales from inside the subject subdivision, PUD
elements.label: text

elements.text: or project, contract sales from inside the subject subdivision, PUD or project may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be used to satisfy this requirement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The use of contract sales must be in addition to the three actual closed sales from
elements.label: text

elements.text: outside the subject subdivision, PUD or project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the subject subdivision, PUD or project is so new that a closed sale or a contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale is not available, comparable sales from outside the subject subdivision, PUD
elements.label: text

elements.text: or project may be used.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In this case, the appraisal report must contain commentary on the marketability of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the new subdivision, PUD or project and justification supporting the use of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales from outside the new subdivision, PUD or project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. At least one comparable sale from outside the subject subdivision, PUD or project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resales from inside the subject subdivision or project are preferable as comparable sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: and should be given significant consideration, as they provide a reliable indicator of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value of units within the subdivision, PUD or project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least two comparable sales must be sales in which the builder or developer of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject property was not involved in the sale transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5705.8 for specific requirements regarding selection of comparable sales for
elements.label: text

elements.text: units in a Cooperative Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verifying completion for new or proposed construction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Verifying completion for an existing property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal reports or PDRs requiring an inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Verifying completion for new or proposed construction
elements.label: text

elements.text: For appraisal reports of new or proposed construction (i.e., the construction has not started
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the description of construction to be completed is based on plans and specifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or similar documentation) that are subject to completion, Freddie Mac will accept one of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following to verify completion of the subject property:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 442, Appraisal Update and/or Completion Report, prepared by an appraiser
elements.label: text

elements.text: documenting the property has been completed in accordance with the plans and
elements.label: text

elements.text: specifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Freddie Mac will accept a completion report prepared by an unlicensed appraiser or
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser trainee (or similar classification) if a supervisory appraiser signs the
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The completion report may be based on an on-site visual inspection or by using
elements.label: text

elements.text: technology to perform a virtual inspection of the subject property to evidence
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 400, Warranty of Completion of Construction or Repairs/Alterations, or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: substantially similar form, signed by the Borrower and the builder confirming the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: property has been completed in accordance with the plans and specifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ By delivering the signed completion form to Freddie Mac, the Seller represents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warrants the subject property has been completed in accordance with the plans and
elements.label: text

elements.text: specifications and there were no material or significant changes to the plans and
elements.label: text

elements.text: specifications after completion of the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Seller is unable to obtain Form 400 signed by both the Borrower and builder,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then Form 442 completed by the appraiser is required
elements.label: text

elements.text: For appraisal reports of existing properties that are subject to repairs or alterations,
